title: nymphaeaceae
intro: The Nymphaeaceae rank is a family of aquatic plants, commonly known as water lilies. This family comprises approximately 70 species of plants that are native to tropical and temperate regions around the world. Members of the Nymphaeaceae family are characterized by their large, flat leaves and showy, fragrant flowers that float on the water's surface. They are often found in ponds, lakes, and slow-moving streams, and play a vital role in supporting aquatic ecosystems.
